item.title: Iran shuts down Strait of Hormuz again, demanding end to US blockade
item.description: Crucial waterway returns to its ‘previous state’, say Iran’s armed forces

item.title: US Supreme Court sides with Chevron in $745 million coastal lawsuit
item.description: Unanimous ruling opens door for case to be retried in federal venue

item.title: WPC Energy Congress reveals new dates after delay due to Middle East war
item.description: Massive petroleum conference rescheduled for October in Saudi Arabia

item.title: US feds plan oil and gas lease sale for Alaska nature reserve
item.description: Bureau of Land Management will put blocks in Alaskan nature reserve up for auction this June

item.title: Petrobras boosts Africa expansion with new block in Sao Tome & Principe
item.description: Brazilian company also has stakes in South Africa and Namibia blocks

item.title: TotalEnergies ‘on track’ with Suriname’s first FPSO as hull enters new yard
item.description: Production from country's first major deepwater project will begin in mid-2028

item.title: Trump says Strait of Hormuz is ‘fully’ open for passage, oil prices plunge
item.description: Oil prices plunge following US President Donald Trump's announcement

item.title: Iran keeps loading oil as US hits out at potential buyers
item.description: US brandishes blacklist as it threatens to pursue Iranian tankers all over the globe

item.title: CNPC takes key role in acreage with high hopes for Kazakhstan
item.description: Chinese company will pay for seismic and exploration drilling on a large onshore block close to its existing Kazakh operations

item.title: Petrobras weighs up 25 new bids after ‘irreparable flaws’ in first tender
item.description: New bidding competition could result in contract awards worth more than $320 million

item.title: EQT executive: ‘Prices are only going to get more volatile’
item.description: Operators must manage unpredictability through well design and inventory, says US gas producer’s upstream chief

item.title: White House calls for more US drilling to flatten oil prices
item.description: Crude prices are up by 48% this year on the Iran war

item.title: Russia remains sticking point as Hungary eyes fresh start
item.description: New Hungarian prime minister promises stronger alignment with European Union, but not when it comes to phasing out Russian energy

item.title: Russian energy remains sticking point as Hungary eyes fresh start
item.description: New Hungarian prime minister promises stronger alignment with European Union, but not when it comes to phasing out Russian energy

item.title: Leading industry veterans make splash in US Gulf with launch of new companmy
item.description: Newly formed 1947 Oil & Gas to focus on building and operating production portfolio in US

item.title: Leading industry veterans make splash in US Gulf with launch of new company
item.description: Newly formed 1947 Oil & Gas to focus on building and operating production portfolio in US

item.title: Ex Talos CEO launches new US Gulf company
item.description: Newly formed 1947 Oil & Gas to focus on building and operating production portfolio in US

item.title: Financial relief for stressed Norwegian E&P company
item.description: Lime Petroleum's troubles started with a major cost blowout on the Seme development

item.title: Namibia onshore project could be on stream in 2028
item.description: ReconAfrica next month to begin critical production tests on Kavango West discovery

item.title: Australia’s billion-dollar LNG projects advance as gas shortages loom
item.description: Feed gas demand from liquefied natural gas exporters increasingly competing with consumption in domestic market

item.title: Petronas steps up carbon capture offshore Malaysia
item.description: McDermott to transport and install key structures for Kasawari carbon capture and storage project

item.title: Petronas decommissioning tender advances
item.description: Decommissioning tender attracting interest from major regional players

item.title: Sinopec to step up domestic natural gas development
item.description: Chinese state giant shifts investment focus from Tianjin LNG terminal construction project to natural gas development in southwestern China

item.title: Inpex moves forward with carbon capture drilling
item.description: Japanese operator gains government approval for Metropolitan CCS joint venture

item.title: Decision nears on expansion work at world's second-largest offshore oilfield
item.description: New expansion phase at Upper Zakum aims to scale up the field’s oil production capacity to up to 1.5 million bpd

item.title: This week's leading oil and gas appointments
Damaged offshore facilities keep Australian oilfield shut down
item.description: Jadestone Energy still evaluating extent of damage at Stag following Cyclone Narelle

item.title: Why a ‘wave of decommissioning’ is heading for the US Gulf
item.description: More operators paying attention to removal strategies as platforms reach end of their production lives

item.title: Drillship contract signed for five wells in Mediterranean Sea
item.description: Transcocean's Deepwater Asgard is contracted for five wells

item.title: Repsol signs deal with Venezuela to increase its production by 50%
item.description: Spanish company is largest European oil operator in Venezuela
